violations.text: Turkey meat was observed marked with a date that exceeded the 7-day limit for TCS foods. The turkey meat was marked with discard date 11/3/25, the date of this inspection is 11/10/25. The turkey meat was voluntarily disposed of during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/10/2025

The following equipment were observed in poor repair or improper adjustment: 1) Pan cover was observed screwed onto fan inside of a reach in freezer unit to catch condensate drip 2) The door handle of the walk-in freezer was broken New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) The storage areas on the main cook line had food residue and other debris 2) The fan covers of the walk-in cooler had an accumulation of dust New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The floor throughout the kitchen was in poor repair (grout observed in poor condition or lacking). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The breakfast prep unit was observed with an ambient temperature of 43 degrees. The lowboy unit has not been serviced yet. Per PIC both units have had service orders placed. Units are not in use. The hand sink in the bathroom was observed without running cold water. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The floors in the cookline area is constructed of worn down grout which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. An accumulation of food debris was observed in the worn down grout. Grout to be redone. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (vegetable slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was taken back to dishwasher area to be sanitized. Inspector and PIC reviewed cleaning procedures for equipment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

Various foods were observed held at 42-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods in breakfast prep unit were recently stocked and moved back to walk-in to cool down. The sauce in the lowboy unit were voluntarily disposed of (stored over night at 60 degrees) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

Utensils at ice cream station were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. The scoops were stored in a warmer unit that was not turned on. Utensils were taken back to dishwasher area to be sanitized. Equipment to only be stored in warmer unit if turned on. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

-The breakfast prep unit was observed iced over with an ambient temperature of 43 degrees. -The lowboy unit was observed at an ambient temperature of 60 degrees. Both units were taken out of service and shall not be used until refrigeration technician services units. -One hand sink in the men's bathroom was observed without running cold water. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. Establishment to monitor temperatures of units after units get serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

The condensate from the cold drawer refrigeration unit near the wall is not draining properly and is pooling in unit. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The floors in the walk-in refrigerator, freezer, and around prep sink drain has an accumulation of food and miscellaneous debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

The floors in the cookline area is constructed of worn down grout which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. An accumulation of food debris was observed in the worn down grout. Grout to be redone. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023
